mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not significantly exceed baseline when FEDFUNDS declines over consecutive months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or flat when UNRATE sustains values below 5.0% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising residential housing starts signals robust aggregate demand and business investment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 40% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, a sharp contraction in real consumer credit forces deleveraging, which suppresses corporate revenue growth and drives negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive when FEDFUNDS rises and RSAFS contracts simultaneously over consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP expands the corporate earnings base, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as earnings revisions propagate.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 shows positive sequential growth.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 for two consecutive months, sustained job creation validates economic momentum, triggering positive equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 200000 in two consecutive months
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 200,000 for two straight months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling consumer credit indicates a shift from debt-fueled consumption to productive capacity expansion, which supports equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction of rising INDPRO and falling RSAFS fails to generate positive 20-day forward returns in more than 45% of observed periods.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating inflation, a rapid rise in the CPI index compresses real yields and triggers equity risk premium repricing, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL accelerates above the specified threshold for two consecutive months.
